name: Szechuan Soup
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon Chinese chili paste or sauce
6 ounces Boneless lean pork, cut into pieces
4 ounces Cooked ham cut the same way
1 small Red bell pepper, cut thin
recipeInstructions:
1. Place mushrooms in a bowl and cover with hot water. Let stand for 30 minutes. Drain and squeeze out excess water.
2. Remove and discard stems. Cut caps into thin slices.
3. In a Dutch oven, combine chicken stock, wine, soy sauce, and chili paste. Bring to boil over medium heat.
4. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes.
5. Blend cornstarch and 1 tablespoon water in a small bowl. Slowly stir the cornstarch mixture into the chicken broth. Cook and stir until the soup boils.
6. Add mushrooms, pork, ham, red bell pepper, and water chestnuts. Simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes.
7. Stir in vinegar and sesame oil. Beat egg and remaining 1 tablespoon water with a fork. Stirring constantly, gradually drizzle the egg mixture slowly into the soup.
8. Add onions, bean curd, and shrimp. Cook until shrimp turn pink, 1-2 minutes. Makes 6-8 servings.
